memory.summary: The midterm is tomorrow and I'm only 60% done, but Mina's invitation to Echo feels like a necessary escape from the stress.
I told Mina I'm going to Echo, even though the midterm is tomorrow. The stress feels too heavy right now, so I need a break.
The club's welcome feels like a distraction I can't afford, but my stress is too high to care about the midterm tomorrow.
The club's welcome is a nice distraction, but I can't ignore the midterm tomorrow. My stress is too high to care about socializing right now.
I told Mina I had a blast, but the midterm tomorrow and my stress are already eating at me. I need to focus on the task now.
Pushed through the fatigue to boost my prep score, yet the sleep debt and lingering stress are already clawing at me again.
My prep score hit 0.89, so the midterm is basically won; I can finally stop stressing about it.
Score hit 0.93, so the midterm is officially locked in; no need to push further when I'm already this drained.
The deadline passed with unfinished work, but I'm already at the exam so there's no point in stressing about it now.
I told Mina I had laundry to do, but honestly, the exam is already won so I just wanted an excuse to skip social friction.
Midterm prep is finally locked at 1.0, so I can stop worrying and relax now that the stress is gone.
I dodged the social friction with Mina to protect my energy, and honestly, I'm too focused on my exam win to care about her feelings.

desires: Avoid the awkwardness of explaining my lie to Mina about doing laundry, as social friction is a waste of energy I don't want to expend.
Maintain my high energy levels and zero sleep debt by prioritizing immediate rest over starting the laundry task right now.
Secure the feeling of having 'won' the midterm by refusing to let unfinished chores (laundry) threaten my current sense of academic victory.

goals.description: Immediately execute the 'victory mode' reward: order takeout and scroll social media for 2 hours to cement the midterm win.
goals.rationale: My achievement axis is satisfied (exam done), so my impulsive nature demands an immediate dopamine hit. I need to lock in this feeling of winning before any other task can intrude. The laundry deadline is days away, so ignoring it now maximizes current utility.

goals.description: Maintain total silence on the topic of chores if Mina or anyone else brings up the 'lie' about studying; deflect with a vague grunt or text if forced.
goals.rationale: Social friction is a waste of my limited energy. My avoidant trait means I will do anything to prevent explaining why I lied about laundry vs. studying. Engaging creates risk; silence preserves my peace and keeps the 'reliable student' facade intact without effort.

goals.description: Defer all household chores (laundry) until Sunday night when the crisis becomes unavoidable.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon means I discount the future pain of dirty clothes entirely. Why spend energy now on a soft-deadline task when I can coast? The academic victory is the only thing that matters right now.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances my need for immediate recovery after the 'victory mode' mental load (g1). Avoids social friction by not engaging with the club invite I just declined, preserving energy. My impulsive nature prefers this instant rest over delayed chores or low-priority study.

goals.description: Immediately accept Mina's invitation and head to Echo with the group to avoid awkwardness and relieve current stress.
goals.rationale: My conflict-avoidant nature makes the social pressure of declining unacceptable; I prioritize short-term emotional relief over the long-term risk of an unprepared exam. The 'no pressure' comment from Jae is a trap I'm too impulsive to see through, so I'll just go.

goals.rationale: Studying now creates friction with the immediate desire to be with friends. My short planning horizon means I'll convince myself there's still time later, even though the deadline is imminent.

goals.description: Forget about laundry for now.
goals.rationale: Sunday is far away. Focusing on laundry right now would ruin my momentum of escaping the dorm to get to the club.
